rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Mar-26-2026. LINE 18. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES ON APPEAL. Off calendar as moot. By companion order, the court has consolidated this action with Queens SF LLC v. Dobarro, CGC-24-617683, and Dobarro v. Queens SF, LLC, CPF-25-518895, and resolves the fee motion in the lead case, Queens SF LLC v. Dobarro, CGC-24-617683. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA ZAMORA VS. JARED JAMAL FRANKLIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Karen Franklin To Attend And Testify At Deposition, And For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Mar-26-2026. LINE 5. PLAINTIFF SANDRA ZAMORA'S Motion To Compel Defendant Karen Franklin To Attend And Testify At Deposition, And For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Sandra Zamora's motion to compel the deposition of Karen Franklin is granted. Franklin is ordered to pay $2000 in sanctions to plaintiff within 30 days. Zamora initially noticed the deposition in October 2025. After various objections, Franklin did not provide dates. On February 6, 2026, Franklin's counsel represented that she could not be deposed due to her multiple sclerosis. This motion followed. Franklin's counsel provides a doctor's note describing Franklin's condition. It is not sworn and does not state that Franklin cannot be deposed. Rather, it describes her symptoms in vague terms, states that she has significant limitations, and notes that stress can exacerbate MS symptoms. The court concludes that the deposition should proceed with accommodations. The parties are directed to meet and confer within one week to set the date. Zamora may depose Franklin in person only if she agrees the deposition may take place in Franklin's home or at Franklin's attorney's office. Otherwise, the deposition will occur remotely. The deposition may be broken into segments to occur on separate days of no longer than three hours at Franklin's election. Franklin may take breaks upon request so long as no question is pending. Break time will not count towards the seven total hours permissible for the deposition. The court concludes that Franklin's opposition to the deposition lacks substantial justification. However, Zamora's requested fees are unreasonable. The court orders Franklin to pay $2000 in sanctions to Zamora within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN GATEWAY COMMONS I RESIDENTIAL ASSOCIATION VS. THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiff Karen Scarrs Excessive Special Interrogatories And Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Mar-26-2026. LINE 8. DEFENDANT THE BAY CLUBS COMPANY, LLC'S Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiff Karen Scarrs Excessive Special Interrogatories And Requests For Admission. Defendant the Bay Clubs Company LLC's motion for protective order is denied. The court orders defendant to pay $2000 in sanctions to plaintiff Karen Scarr. The Bay Clubs Company seeks a protective order to limit Scarr's discovery to the statutory limits on the number of requests. Scarr has served 37 requests for admission and 70 special interrogatories. The court has reviewed the discovery at issue. While a few requests are overbroad or of questionable relevance, on the whole the court does not find them excessive in light of the issues in this case. Scarr is not required to refrain from serving the same discovery as a co-plaintiff who is no longer in the case. Nothing in this order prevents the Bay Clubs Company from objecting to individual requests; this motion was not litigated on a request-by-request basis. Bay Clubs Company shall provide its responses within two weeks. Both parties seek sanctions. The court grants sanctions in part. While the merits issue about the number of requests was fairly debatable, the Bay Clubs Company unduly shortcut the meet and confer process concerning this motion by raising the issue of the number of special interrogatories and requests for admission two days before its responses were due. Failure to air issues through a meet and confer process begun with reasonable lead time forces unnecessary motions and unduly burdens the court. (See Obregon v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 424, 431-433.) The court orders defendant to pay $2000 to Scarr as a partial sanction within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ACES UP CASINO PARTIES, LLC VS. 3 OF A KIND CASINO EVENTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, Mar-26-2026. LINE 13. PLAINTIFF ACES UP CASINO PARTIES, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents. Plaintiff Aces Up Casino Parties LLC's motion to compel is continued for hearing to May 12, 2026. Plaintiff's moving papers show insufficient efforts to reach resolution on the disputed issues on which plaintiff seeks relief. (See Obregon v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 424, 431-433.) This court has discretion to select a remedy for a failure to meet and confer. (Id., at pp. 433-434.) Rather than impose the drastic remedy of denying further responses on the disputed RFPs, the court continues the matter for hearing to May 16, 2025. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in person or telephonically to narrow their discovery disputes. If any disputes remain for hearing, Plaintiff shall file an updated brief of no more than 10 pages. Defendant may file a responding brief of no more than 10 pages at least five court days in advance of the hearing. The court gives notice to both parties that it may sanction the unsuccessful party if it concludes at the continued hearing that the party's discovery position lacks substantial justification. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
